title: Local file context integration
description: File picker via Tauri dialog plugin. Read file contents. Context card rendering with filename, first few lines preview, relative size bar. Card expand/reorder/remove. Optional watch mode via notify crate. Split from mood-editor-ona.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: File card component renders filename, preview, size bar correctly. Card reorder updates sort_order.
- Integration: Pick file via dialog mock → verify content read and cached. Watch file → modify → verify cache update event fires.
- Manual: File picker opens native dialog. Card preview shows useful first lines. Size bar proportions make sense. Expand/collapse is smooth.

title: Reflex — Ambient AI Margin Annotations
description: A persistent, spatial AI layer that reads alongside you as you write and populates the right margin with brief, contextual annotations. Analyses each paragraph via fast model (Haiku/Flash), surfaces consistency checks, cross-note connections, continuity observations, and structural nudges. Annotations are spatial, ambient, cross-note aware, context-aware, and ephemeral. Build items 19-24 in plan.md.

title: Reflex backend: analysis engine + IPC
description:
Rust module (src-tauri/src/reflex.rs) with:
- ReflexEngine struct managing a dedicated pi session for fast model calls (Haiku 3.5 or Gemini Flash)
- analyze_paragraph IPC command: accepts paragraph text, surrounding paragraphs, document mode/tag, returns Vec<Annotation>
- Structured output prompt returning JSON array of annotations (type, message, confidence, optional ref)
- Annotation types: consistency, connection, continuity, structure, question
- Paragraph content-hash cache (SHA-256 of trimmed text -> cached annotations) to avoid re-analyzing unchanged paragraphs
- Cache TTL of 30 minutes, invalidation on context item changes
- Global rate limiting: max 1 analysis request per 2 seconds, queue excess
- toggle_reflex IPC command for enable/disable state
- JSON parse validation with fallback to empty annotations on malformed output

Acceptance criteria:
- analyze_paragraph returns valid annotations for test paragraph
- Cache hit returns instantly without model call
- Rate limiter queues excess requests
- Malformed model output returns empty array, not error
- Unit tests for cache, rate limiter, and prompt construction

title: Reflex backend: cross-note + context awareness
description:
Extend ReflexEngine with cross-note and context-aware analysis:
- FTS query against notes_fts to find related notes for a given paragraph (top 3-5 by relevance)
- Include related note titles + excerpts in the analysis prompt
- Check paragraph claims against loaded context items (files, GitHub, Notion content)
- Include relevant context item excerpts in the analysis prompt
- Adjust prompt based on document tag/mode (#blog -> style focus, #plan -> structure focus)
- Feed assembled context into the structured output prompt from task 19

Acceptance criteria:
- FTS query returns relevant notes for test paragraph content
- Context items are included in analysis prompt when loaded
- Mode-specific prompt variations produce different annotation styles
- Unit tests for FTS query construction and context assembly

title: CM6 Reflex extension: paragraph tracking + debounce
description:
New CM6 extension (src/editor/inline-reflex.ts) with:
- StateField tracking per-paragraph annotation state: Map from paragraph index to Annotation[]
- StateField for enabled/disabled toggle
- ViewPlugin that watches doc changes and identifies which paragraphs changed
- 2-3 second debounce per paragraph after last edit before triggering analysis
- IPC call to analyze_paragraph backend command with paragraph text + surrounding context
- StateEffect for: setAnnotations, clearAnnotations, toggleReflex
- Paragraph boundary detection (split on double newline or heading markers)
- Cancel in-flight analysis when paragraph is edited again (abort previous debounce)
- Frontend API bindings (src/api/reflex.ts) for analyze_paragraph and toggle_reflex

Acceptance criteria:
- Paragraph changes trigger debounced analysis after 2-3s of inactivity
- Editing a paragraph cancels pending analysis for that paragraph
- Annotations update in StateField when analysis returns
- Toggle enables/disables all analysis
- Tests for debounce logic, paragraph detection, and state management

title: Margin annotation rendering
description:
CM6 gutter widgets for Reflex annotations (extends inline-reflex.ts):
- Right gutter using Decoration.widget for each annotation, aligned to its source paragraph
- Annotation type icons: checkmark (consistency), arrow (connection), ellipsis (continuity), tilde (structure), question mark (question)
- Faded warm text styling (text-4 opacity, 0.75rem font size) matching the warm dark theme
- Single-line rendering, truncated with ellipsis. Hover to show full annotation in a tooltip
- Fade-in animation (200ms) when annotations arrive, fade-out when invalidated
- Subtle vertical separator line between document text and margin
- Margin collapses to zero width when Reflex is disabled
- Clickable connection annotations that could navigate to referenced notes (emit event)
- CSS in src/index.css for .reflex-annotation, .reflex-gutter, .reflex-fade-in/out

Acceptance criteria:
- Annotations render in right margin aligned to correct paragraphs
- Each annotation type displays correct icon
- Hover shows full text for truncated annotations
- Fade-in/out transitions work on annotation add/remove
- Margin hidden when Reflex disabled
- Tests for widget rendering and style classes

title: Reflex integration: toggle, mode awareness, wiring
description:
Wire Reflex into Page.tsx and the broader app:
- Ctrl+/ keymap in Page.tsx to toggle Reflex on/off
- Persist toggle state in SQLite settings table (key: reflex_enabled)
- Footer bar indicator: subtle glyph that shows warm when active, muted when off
- Tag-based mode awareness: pass current note tag to analysis, disable in #chat mode
- Mode-specific behavior: #blog -> style annotations, #plan -> structural, #scratch -> connections only, #tweet -> character/tone, #chat -> disabled
- Wire context item changes (add/remove from tray) to cache invalidation via IPC
- Wire note tag changes to Reflex mode update
- Add Reflex extension to createBaseExtensions() in extensions.ts with proper options
- Keyboard shortcut added to README shortcuts table

Acceptance criteria:
- Ctrl+/ toggles Reflex on/off, state persists across sessions
- Footer indicator reflects current Reflex state
- #chat mode disables Reflex automatically
- Context item changes invalidate annotation cache
- Reflex extension loads in editor with proper wiring
- Tests for toggle, mode filtering, and Page integration

title: Reflex tests
description:
Comprehensive test coverage for Reflex feature:

Rust tests (src-tauri/src/reflex.rs):
- Paragraph hash cache: insert, hit, miss, TTL expiry, invalidation
- Rate limiter: allows within limit, queues excess, resets after interval
- Prompt construction: mode-specific prompts, context inclusion, FTS results included
- JSON parse: valid annotations, malformed JSON fallback, empty response handling

Frontend tests:
- inline-reflex StateField: paragraph tracking, annotation updates, toggle
- Debounce: fires after delay, cancels on re-edit, no fire when disabled
- Paragraph detection: splits on double newline, handles headings, edge cases
- Widget rendering: correct annotation types, truncation, fade classes
- Page integration: Ctrl+/ toggle, mode awareness, footer indicator state
- API bindings: mock IPC for analyze_paragraph and toggle_reflex

Acceptance criteria:
- All Rust unit tests pass (cargo test)
- All frontend tests pass (pnpm test)
- Minimum 30 new test cases across both layers
- Edge cases covered: empty document, single paragraph, rapid edits, disabled state

title: Light theme: CSS variable overrides + CM6 adaptation
description:
Add light theme support via CSS custom property overrides:
- Add .theme-light class in src/index.css overriding all --color-surface-*, --color-text-*, --color-accent-*, --color-status-* variables with warm paper-white palette (see plan.md Light Theme section for exact values)

- Text colors: rich darks (#2c2a26), not pure black
- Accents: slightly deeper versions for contrast on light backgrounds
- Make CM6 moodTheme dark flag dynamic: export a function that creates theme with dark:true or dark:false based on current theme. Or use two theme instances swapped via compartment.
- Scrollbar thumb needs to be visible on light background (use --color-accent-muted override)
- Selection highlight adapts automatically (already uses --color-accent-warm with mix)
- All inline AI/invoke/conversation CSS already uses var() — verify they look correct on light
- ::selection pseudo-element adapts via variable

Acceptance criteria:
- Adding .theme-light class to body switches entire app to light palette
- CM6 editor renders correctly with light theme (selections, cursor, code blocks)
- All inline AI elements (invoke line, generated text, conversation widgets) are legible on light

title: Fix conversation forking to use pi-native session fork
description:
Current implementation stringifies conversation history into append_system_prompt for a new session. This destroys conversation structure (model sees flat text not turns), loses JSONL DAG continuity, has no parent_session linking, and cannot switch back to the original branch.

Should use pi's native fork: add get_fork_messages and fork commands to the PiBridge that mirror the RPC handler logic — plan_fork_from_user_message on the Session, create new JSONL with shared entries, swap the active session in-place. The SQLite note linking (forked_from) is fine, but the pi session manipulation must go through the SDK.

Revert the app-level fork implementation (commit 703ad38) and replace with native pi fork.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Bridge fork command creates new JSONL with shared entries up to fork point. get_fork_messages returns forkable entry IDs.
- Integration: Fork at message 3 of 5 → new session shares first 3 entries via JSONL DAG, not string duplication. Both branches continuable. parent_session set correctly.
- Manual: Ctrl+B forks, old branch accessible via library, new branch continues from fork point.

title: Fix context injection to use pi extensions instead of message appending
description:
Current implementation appends assembled context set content to the user's message string in useConversation (messageWithContext = trimmed + contextStr). The LLM sees context as part of every user message.

Problems:
- Bloats every user message with repeated context (context re-sent on every turn)
- Makes context invisible to pi's session history (buried in message text)
- Breaks on re-send/edit — context duplicated or lost
- Wastes tokens since context is repeated in each message, not in system prompt

The plan explicitly specifies context sets should be injected via pi extensions (enable_extensions_with_policy) or at minimum via append_system_prompt on the session. Pi extensions can inject system-level context that persists across turns without polluting user messages.

Fix: Remove message-appending from useConversation. Set append_system_prompt when creating/connecting the session with assembled context, or implement a pi extension that reads from SQLite. Update when context items change (may require session reconnect or a set_system_prompt bridge command).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Context content is in system prompt, not in user message text.
- Integration: Send 3 messages with context set active → verify context appears once in system prompt, not 3x in user messages.
- Manual: Context tray items influence AI responses without appearing in conversation message bubbles.

title: Claude conversation import
description: Parse Claude JSON export into pi JSONL sessions + SQLite note records.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: JSON parser handles Claude export format. Message structure preserved (user/assistant alternation). Timestamps mapped correctly. Malformed JSON returns clear error.
- Integration: Import real Claude export file → verify note count matches conversations. Verify each note has pi_session JSONL. Verify #imported/claude tag. Verify FTS indexes imported content.
- Manual: Import completes in reasonable time for 100+ conversations. Imported conversations are browsable and continuable.

title: Fix distill flow to actually run AI synthesis instead of saving raw prompt
description:
Current implementation builds a synthesis prompt with buildDistillPrompt() and saves it as the new note's content. It never sends it to the AI. The user opens a note that literally contains 'Distill the following conversation into a plan document...' with the raw conversation pasted in.

Should: Create a pi session, send the distill prompt, stream the AI's synthesized output, and save the AI's response as the document content. The distill flow should show a streaming indicator while generating, then save the final output.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Distill sends prompt to pi session and accumulates AI response as note content.
- Integration: Distill a 5-message conversation → new note contains AI-synthesized document, not the raw prompt template.
- Manual: Cmd+D shows streaming indicator, resulting document is well-structured AI output with proper formatting.

title: Tag-based mode switching and templates
description: Tag-based mode switching: #chat/#plan/#blog/#tweet/#scratch. Templates, contextual Enter.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: Tag parser extracts #chat/#plan/#blog/#tweet from first line. Mode resolver returns correct Enter behavior per tag. Template loader returns correct template per tag.
- Integration: Type #plan as first word in new note → verify template loads and Enter inserts newline. Type #chat → verify Enter sends message.
- Manual: Mode switch feels instant. Template content is useful. No visual jarring on mode transition.

title: Context tray UI shell
description: Slide-up panel from bottom of window (like iOS Control Center). Translucent overlay, writing visible behind. Drag/push down to dismiss. Tiny context count badge in bottom margin. No content integration yet — just the animated panel shell. Split from mood-editor-ona.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): Tray component state transitions (closed/opening/open/closing). Context count badge updates from prop.
- Manual: Drag-up gesture feels like iOS Control Center. Translucent overlay — writing visible behind. Push-down dismisses smoothly. Animation is 150-200ms. No scroll interference with main content.

title: Conversation message rendering and layout
description: Display user and AI messages with distinct styling (weight/opacity). Scroll behavior, conversation structure. No bubbles, no avatars — dialogue flows like prose. Static rendering of message history from pi session. Split from mood-editor-kby.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): Message component renders user vs AI messages with correct styling classes. Empty conversation shows placeholder.
- Integration: Load a pi JSONL session file, verify all messages render in correct order with correct roles.
- Manual: Verify visual weight distinction between user and AI text is readable. Scroll behavior feels natural with long conversations.

title: Conversation rendering and message flow
description: Subscribe to pi events. Render conversation messages on page. Enter-to-send.

title: Hydrate conversation messages from pi session instead of React-only state
description:
Current implementation maintains conversation messages entirely in React state (useState in useConversation), accumulated from streaming events. It never reads from pi's session. Problems:

- On page reload or note switch, messages are lost (pi has them in JSONL, but the hook doesn't hydrate from there)
- Fork/branch operations depend on React state, not the authoritative pi session state
- Conversation as displayed may drift from what pi actually has (after compaction, steering, aborts)
- Loading an existing conversation note shows empty messages even though the JSONL has full history

The authoritative message list should come from handle.messages() (pi's to_messages_for_current_path()). React state can cache for rendering performance, but should hydrate from pi session on note load and reconcile after operations like fork/compact.

Requires: Add a get_messages bridge command that returns the current session's message list. useConversation hydrates from this on connect/reconnect. Streaming events still update incrementally for real-time display.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Loading an existing conversation note hydrates messages from pi session JSONL.
- Integration: Create conversation with 5 messages → switch to another note → switch back → all 5 messages visible (hydrated from session, not lost).
- Manual: Reopening an old conversation shows full history. Messages survive page reload.

title: Hybrid inline Markdown rendering with cursor-aware toggle
description: CodeMirror 6 extension for hybrid inline rendering: headings render as headings, bold as bold, code blocks with syntax highlighting. When cursor enters a Markdown element, raw syntax appears. When cursor leaves, it re-renders. Progressive enhancement on top of basic CM6 setup. Split from mood-editor-2y0.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): CM6 extension produces correct decorations for headings, bold, code blocks. Cursor enter/leave toggles raw syntax.
- Manual: Open editor, type markdown with h1/h2/bold/code/links. Move cursor through each element — verify raw syntax appears at cursor, renders away when cursor leaves. Check no flicker on fast cursor movement.

title: Distribution — Cross-Platform Installers
description: Set up cross-platform installer generation and CI/CD release pipeline. Covers bundle config for deb/AppImage/dmg/msi/nsis, GitHub Actions workflow with build matrix, and code signing documentation. Build items in Distribution section of plan.md.

title: Bundle config: platform-specific installer options
description:
Update src-tauri/tauri.conf.json bundle section with platform-specific config:
- Linux deb: depends (libgtk-3-0, libwebkit2gtk-4.1-0), section (editors)
- Linux AppImage: bundleMediaFramework false
- Windows NSIS: oneClick false, perMachine false, installerIcon
- macOS DMG: windowSize, appPosition, applicationFolderPosition
- macOS: minimumSystemVersion 10.15
- Verify icons exist for all formats (already present: .png, .icns, .ico)
- Test local build on current platform with 'pnpm tauri build'

Acceptance criteria:
- tauri.conf.json has linux, windows, macOS bundle sections
- 'pnpm tauri build' succeeds on current platform and produces installer
- Bundle output appears in src-tauri/target/release/bundle/

title: GitHub Actions release workflow
description:
Create .github/workflows/release.yml with:
- Trigger on tag push (v*)
- Build matrix: ubuntu-22.04, macos-latest (universal-apple-darwin), windows-latest
- Steps: checkout, setup node, setup pnpm, install Rust stable, install Linux deps (apt), install frontend deps, tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
- Rust targets for macOS universal: aarch64-apple-darwin + x86_64-apple-darwin
- Draft release with version from tauri.conf.json
- GITHUB_TOKEN for release creation
- Document release workflow in README: bump version -> tag -> push -> review draft -> publish

Acceptance criteria:
- Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/release.yml
- YAML is valid and references correct action versions
- Linux step installs libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev and other Tauri v2 deps
- macOS step builds universal binary
- README documents the release process

title: Code signing documentation + secrets setup
description:
Document code signing requirements and setup:
- macOS: Apple Developer cert, notarization env vars (APPLE_CERTIFICATE, APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_ID, APPLE_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID), how to export .p12 cert, how to add as GitHub secrets
- Windows: TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY, EV cert options, SmartScreen implications
- Linux: note that signing is not required
- Add docs/signing.md with step-by-step instructions
- Add placeholder secrets references in release.yml (commented out, ready to uncomment)

Acceptance criteria:
- docs/signing.md exists with clear per-platform instructions
- release.yml has commented-out signing env vars ready to enable
- README links to signing docs

title: Context tray with local file support
description: Context tray from bottom. Universal search (files, repos, notes, URLs). Cards with preview.

title: Live test inline AI generation end-to-end
description: The invoke → generate pipeline is wired but hasn't been tested against a live pi agent session. Needs manual verification: Tab at line start → type instruction → Enter → stream text → preview styling → Enter to accept / Esc to dismiss. May need fixes for edge cases (empty responses, errors, cursor position after accept).

title: Inline document AI generation
description: Tab-to-invoke inline AI in document mode. Inline instruction, generation, accept/dismiss.
